holdout: ADVERSARIAL — never include in train or val splits. Used in evaluate.py as the unseen-provider generalisation gate.

examples.notes: ADVERSARIAL HOLDOUT — Replicate predictions: completely different shape from chat-completions. `output` is a single string (or list, model-dependent) holding the assistant reply. `version` is the immutable model-version hash (Replicate's content-addressed model identifier) → model_hash. Three timestamps (created_at/started_at/completed_at) — only created_at gets the canonical response_timestamp; started/completed are queue-internal milestones (UNKNOWN). predict_time is a duration → timing_value. logs are stdout (operator artifact, not assistant content) → UNKNOWN.
